ForgotPasswordResponse.description:
Always 200 + this body — never leak whether the email exists.
`reset_token` is populated only in non-production environments so
dev/staging can test the flow without SMTP wired.
ForgotPasswordResponse.title: ForgotPasswordResponse

GoogleLoginRequest.description:
OAuth2 authorization-code flow from the product's frontend.
The frontend gets `code` from Google's redirect and forwards it
here along with the same `redirect_uri` it used.
GoogleLoginRequest.title: GoogleLoginRequest

InviteUserResponse.description:
Returned only from POST /users. Carries the one-shot password so the
inviter can share it out-of-band. Never persisted in plaintext, never
returned from subsequent reads.
InviteUserResponse.title: InviteUserResponse

ProductUpdate.description:
Mutate an existing product. `settings` is shallow-merged on top
of the existing JSONB so partial patches don't wipe unrelated keys
— e.g. updating `auth.refresh_ttl_days` leaves `features.*` alone.
ProductUpdate.title: ProductUpdate

RegisterIndividualRequest.description:
B2C signup. No tenant_name / tenant_slug — the platform derives them.
Creates a private "tenant of 1" with `type=individual`.
RegisterIndividualRequest.title: RegisterIndividualRequest

TenantOwner.description:
Optional owner user to create atomically alongside the tenant.
Admin-only bootstrap path — saves a follow-up call to invite + activate.
TenantOwner.title: TenantOwner

TenantType.description:
B2B vs B2C marker. Behavior is identical under the hood (tenant
is the billing / audit / RBAC boundary); product UIs use this to
render team-aware vs single-user flows.
TenantType.title: TenantType

schema.title: Response List Products Api V1 Admin Products Get

patch.description:
Update fields on an existing product. `settings` is merged on top
of the existing JSONB (keys not in the patch are preserved). Common
use: configure per-product auth (`{"auth": {"refresh_ttl_days": 7}}`)
or feature flags (`{"features": {"google_auto_provision": true}}`).

post.description:
OAuth2 authorization-code login.

Frontend flow:
1. Redirect user to `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth`
   with this product's `client_id`, the `redirect_uri` it'll handle,
   `scope=openid email profile`, and a `state` nonce.
2. Google redirects back to `redirect_uri` with `code` + `state`.
3. Frontend verifies `state`, then POSTs {code, redirect_uri} here.

Returns the standard TokenPair on success. If the email isn't
registered and the product hasn't opted into
`settings.features.google_auto_provision`, returns 401 — admin must
invite the user first.

Always returns 200 with ok=true — never leaks whether the email
exists. In non-production environments the response carries the raw
token so dev/staging can test the flow without SMTP wired.

Production: route the token into a transactional-email provider in
`app/providers/notifications.py` (currently a stub) and build the
public reset link as `<product_app_url>/reset?token=<token>`.

Consumes a forgot-password token, updates the password, and
revokes every existing refresh token so old sessions can't continue.

Convenience endpoint for products whose customer is an individual,
not a company. No tenant_name / tenant_slug required — the platform
derives them. Under the hood it's a normal register with
`type=individual`, so everything (subscription, credits, audit) works
identically. The user can later invite teammates if they want.
post.summary: B2C signup — creates a private tenant of 1

schema.title: Response Ledger Api V1 Credits Ledger Get
Response List Wallets Api V1 Credits Wallets Get
Response List Plans Api V1 Plans Get
Response List Roles Api V1 Roles Get
Response List Permissions Api V1 Roles Permissions Get

get.description: Return the effective tenant + its children. Scope honors act-as.

schema.title: Response List Tenants Api V1 Tenants Get

get.description:
List direct children of the caller's *home* tenant, with a per-child
`can_act_as` flag. UIs use this to render a "switch tenant" picker.

Always evaluated against the home tenant — switching context with
`X-Acting-Tenant-Slug` doesn't change which children you can switch into.

schema.title: Response List Children Api V1 Tenants Children Get
Response List Users Api V1 Users Get
